item.title: Time to face America’s most boring nightmare
item.description: Soaring interest rates and entitlements are driving a massive debt.

item.title: How many chemicals are in your food?
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on chemicals in food.

item.title: Trump’s central plan to boost coal
item.description: The administration is spending hundreds of millions of dollars to subsidize the coal industry.

item.title: From front-runner to Epstein files: The spiral of Nancy Mace
item.description: Trump’s endorsement shocker brings new drama to South Carolina politics.

item.title: Was Trump’s ‘weaponization fund’ doomed from the start?
item.description: Was President Donald Trump's "Anti-Weaponization Fund" doomed to fail from the start? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Kate Andrews, Jason Willick and Damir Marusic discuss the rise and fall of the $1.776 billion fund while exploring whether "lawfare" has fundamentally changed the ideal of an apolitical justice system.

item.title: Forget $250. This is the banknote Trump should appear on.
item.description: Interfaith marriage, Trump money, Iranian terrorism and improv comedy, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Early dismissals and random days off are driving parents and teachers crazy
item.description: School calendars, the Iran war and AI fiction, in readers’ eyes.
